_DRAFT_NOTE: AGENT-DRAFTED 2026-05-06 per SB-095 — DRAFT v1, NOT operator-confirmed. Phase 5a of UX-design pass: fills the 'more minified' tier operator named 2026-05-06 ('we can also create configuration of profiles too ? like one that is more minified ?'). Single-line, 4 widgets, semantic colors per Phase-2 palette. Use case: deep-coding mode where statusline must not distract — only the 4 most-load-bearing signals (Mode + Task + Ctx Used + Blockers).
_USE_CASE: Deep coding / focused implementation — minimum visual distraction. Switch to higher tier (work/planning/review) when context-switching to PM-style work.

_RATIONALE_PER_WIDGET.mode: Identity — which agent persona is active (magenta = identity color per palette).
_RATIONALE_PER_WIDGET.task: Active task cursor with readiness — answers 'what am I working on right now' (brightWhite = focus-headline per palette).
_RATIONALE_PER_WIDGET.ctxpct: Context-window % used — answers 'when do I need to compact' (brightYellow = attention-without-alarm per palette).
_RATIONALE_PER_WIDGET.blockers: Pending operator-decision count — answers 'what's blocked on operator' (red = blocker color per palette).

_NOT_INCLUDED_AND_WHY.objective-layer: Mission/focus/impediment/P1 deferred to higher tiers — minified is for deep-coding when objective-layer is already mentally loaded.
_NOT_INCLUDED_AND_WHY.git-row: Git state available via terminal prompt anyway; 1-line statusline can't carry it.
_NOT_INCLUDED_AND_WHY.telemetry-numbers: Tokens/cost/usage deferred to higher tiers; deep-coding doesn't need spend awareness per-keystroke.
